message:
test(lifecycle): pin cleanup-payload lockstep across status/diag/doctor (bd-ibuuh.19)

Bead \`coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.19\` already has a lockstep
test that proves aggregate quarantine COUNTERS agree across
\`cass status --json\`, \`cass diag --json --quarantine\`, and
\`cass doctor --json --quarantine\`. Counters agreeing is necessary
but not sufficient: cleanup APPROVAL is driven by the full
\`lexical_cleanup_dry_run\` and \`lexical_cleanup_apply_gate\`
payloads, which include the approval fingerprint, the ordered
candidate list, the retained-vs-reclaimable byte breakdown, and
the per-inventory reasons. If one surface emits a stale
fingerprint or a different candidate list, a robot could approve a
plan from one command and then apply a DIFFERENT plan through
another — same aggregate counters, different actual work.

Two changes land together.

\`tests/cli_diag.rs\` — shared fixtures:

- New helper \`seed_two_retained_publish_backups(data_dir)\`
  creates two retained-publish-backup directories
  (\`prior-live-older\` and \`prior-live-newer\`) under
  \`index_parent/.lexical-publish-backups\`, each with one
  segment file. A 20 ms sleep between the two writes pins a
  deterministic older-vs-newer mtime ordering so the retention-
  by-mtime branch in cleanup classification behaves the same
  every run.

- New helper \`run_diag_quarantine_with_retention(test_home,
  data_dir, retention)\` runs \`cass diag --json --quarantine\`
  with the \`CASS_LEXICAL_PUBLISH_BACKUP_RETENTION\` env knob
  set to the supplied string and returns the parsed JSON
  payload. Wraps the \`cargo_bin!\` invocation, the standard
  \`CODING_AGENT_SEARCH_NO_UPDATE_PROMPT=1\`,
  \`CASS_IGNORE_SOURCES_CONFIG=1\`, and the
  \`XDG_DATA_HOME\` / \`XDG_CONFIG_HOME\` / \`HOME\` overrides
  used elsewhere in the crate.

- New helper \`retained_publish_backup_safety_counts(quarantine)\`
  extracts \`(retained_count, reclaim_count)\` from a diag
  quarantine payload so the lockstep test can compare the two
  numbers across surfaces without re-parsing every field.

\`tests/lifecycle_matrix.rs\` — new lockstep test
\`status_diag_and_doctor_cleanup_payloads_stay_in_lockstep\`:

- Seeds a realistic degraded corpus: a failed-baseline-seed
  backup under \`backups/\` plus its matching WAL file, two
  retained publish backups (older + newer, mtime-ordered), and a
  quarantined generation with one segment file using the existing
  \`write_quarantined_generation_manifest\` helper.
- Runs all three operator surfaces (\`status\`, \`diag
  --quarantine\`, \`doctor --quarantine\`) against the same seed
  and extracts each surface's \`lexical_cleanup_dry_run\` and
  \`lexical_cleanup_apply_gate\` blocks.
- Asserts the approval fingerprint is identical across all three,
  the candidate list is the same set of \`generation_id\`s in the
  same order, and the retained / reclaimable byte counters match
  exactly. If any surface drifts from the canonical payload, the
  test fails and names which field diverged.
- Also pins \`retained_publish_backups\` and the \`cleanup_apply\`
  block to the same per-surface equality, so an approve-from-X /
  apply-through-Y robot sequence can't silently retire a plan
  that a different surface never saw.

Pure test additions. No production code or API change.
